steps.title: Use the harmlessness contrast
steps.content: The sentence calls vibration and warming "harmless applications" and then contrasts them with a stimulus that seems "less benign," such as a mild electric shock. The blank should describe the harmless, mild nature of vibration and warming while setting up the surprising idea that those stimuli offer less relief.

steps.title: Choose the harmless word
steps.content:
• Choice A - deceptive: Deceptive means misleading, but the text does not suggest vibration or warming trick anyone.
  • Choice C - novel: Novel means new or original, while the sentence is about whether the stimuli seem harmless and how much relief they provide.
  • Choice D - impractical: Impractical means unsuitable for use, but the text says vibration and warming can provide temporary distraction, so they are usable even if less effective.
The needed word echoes "harmless" and contrasts with "less benign." The answer is B.
